libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No external environment variables or JVM system properties are required. Keep tests deterministic by using MapPropertySource or built-in .properties sources for core decoding coverage. If explicitly testing Hoplite 3 environment/system-property behavior, inject a controlled EnvironmentVariablesPropertySource map supplier or set and clear System properties inside the test rather than relying on ambient CI environment.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8367 [Automation] native-image run fails for com.sksamuel.hoplite:hoplite-core:3.0.0.RC1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Documentation examples often use YAML, JSON, TOML, or HOCON files; those formats require optional hoplite-* parser modules, but they are not required for meaningful hoplite-core coverage and would expand the scope beyond the core artifact.

Hoplite 3 changed default environment-variable property-source behavior, so tests using ConfigLoaderBuilder.default() with broad config classes may accidentally observe ambient CI environment variables unless sources are made explicit.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: hoplite-core 3.0.0.RC1 resolves only Kotlin/coroutines compile dependencies and core exposes meaningful testable APIs via MapPropertySource, built-in Java properties parsing, default decoders, classpath/resource loading, system properties, and environment-property-source constructors; no Docker image, extra Maven module, or externally supplied environment/system property is required before coverage is reachable.
